Apotheosis , or the exalt of the romish bishop above all that be call God . have speak of the injury offer unto the son of God by the romish synagogue , who permit he not to be lord over his own house , thrust officer into his church , such as he never ordain nor know : now let we examine whether the Lucifer of roam lift not up himself above all that be call God . and sure two thing there be which make this our assertion evident to every one that will considerate look into the business . the first be the ambitious title of insolent and intolerable arrogancy which he take to himself : the second be the absolute , unlimited , and lawless power which he usurp . this power and sovereign majesty which he do exercise , be twofold . the one spiritual : the other temporal . the spiritual power he claim be fourfold : the power of expound and discern scripture : power of decree : power of judge : and power of pardon . the temporal power which he exercise , stand in direction , and in destruction . the first be to prescribe and teach king and prince , state and people what they shall do , and how they shall govern : the second consist in the power destructive of their authority , and in the power destructive of their person , by excommunicate and depose : by murder and slay of prince : by discharge subject from their oath and obedience to they . to these end and purpose tend all the power of our Nebuchadnezar : all the thunder decree and decretal , bull and constitution which he send forth . to this end only be all his friar and Jesuit employ in every kingdom and province , in every part and angle of Christendom , Iudg. 9.15 . that the bramble may be make king , and that all the tree may put their trust under his shadow : and if not , the fire shall come out of the bramble , and consume the cedar of Libanus .

Attersoll on the sacrament . 388.389 . four , they turn the sacrament into a sacrifice for the quick and the dead , abolish the fruit and remembrance of the death of Christ , disannul his priesthood , give he to his father , whereas the father have give he to we , etc ibid . page 390. five , they maintain transubstantiation . these be their very word : if any man shall say , that there remain the substance of bread and wine in the sacrament , after the word of consecration , or shall deny that the whole substance of bread and wine be change and convert into the body and blood of Christ ( the form and show only of bread and wine remain : which singular and miraculous conversion the church call , transubstantiation ) : let he be accurse . Con. Trid. Sess. 13. can . 2. this their doctrine of transubstantiation , be a very fable to mock fool withal , and it overturn both the nature and use of the sacrament , Attersoll on the sacrament , page 45. 46. and page 365. to 369. if there be a miraculous conversion ( as they say there be ) of the bread and wine , it will appear to the outward sense . for all true miracle be wrought open , clear , and evident to man sense , John 6.26 . but the bread and wine , by the judgement of all the sense , remain and appear to be the same in substance which it be before , of the same quality quantity , colour , taste , handle , smell , virtue and nourishment : there be not any one sense , or all the sense together , that can judge otherwise of it , than it do before . if a man shall be call in , when the bread and wine be set on the table , and bid to consider well what he there see , smell and taste , and then be will to go forth , and to come in again , after the consecration be end by the priest , and to do the like : and then ▪ be ask what he think of it : he , no doubt , will answer ( unless fear of persecution make he to conceal the truth ) i see , feel , smell , and taste the same water-cake , and wine that i do before . i can perceive no natural and substantial change therein . and therefore it follow , that there be no miracle wrought , and consequent ; no transubstantiation at all . the difference that be , be in the end and use only . before consecration , it be common bread and wine , ordain for the nourish of our body . after consecration , it become holy bread and wine , sanctify by the lord , not so much to feed the body , as the soul . C. do not the ancient father hold this doctrine of transubstantiation ? m. they know nothing hereof , for at least 800. year after Christ . afterwards begin the disputation of transubstantiation , but not approve as a article of faith . the church for a whole thousand year teach no other then spiritual receive of Christ . in the year 1215. transubstantiation be decree and determine in the council of Lateran , under pope innocent the three , and make a main matter of faith , Perk . 2. vol. 558.559 . C. what say you then of their transubstantiate , or consecrate host ( as i• be call ) or the bread in the box carry in procession and worship ? m. sure it be nothing else , but a wheaten , or breaden god , or rather a idol , nothing inferior to Aaron calf , or ieroboam calf , or the Nehustan , and piece of brass that Ezechias break in piece : nay , as vile and detestable , as any idol among the heathen . and for a conclusion of their doctrine of transubstantiation , i will here set down a witty conceit which one show i not long since : i have keep the matter , but change the meet , to make it sound somewhat the sweet . ) the priest do make Christ body and blood , hereof none must once doubt : they eat , they drink , they box he up , they bear he all about . dialogve 7. C. i be satisfy touch the first point , namely , that the papist be not of sound faith : but how do you prove that they be not of good life , see they do so many good work ? m. i prove it thus : where the doctrine be corrupt , the life can be good : but their doctrine ( as you have hear ) be most corrupt , therefore their life can be good . a true faith be the ground of a good life , and without which it be impossible to please God , Heb. 11.6 . yea , whatsoever be not of faith , be sin , Rom. 14.23 . a true faith they have not , and therefore their work can be good , and such as may please God . that they have not a true and sound faith , have be show in many particular , and it further appear also in this , that they do even wilful rey she mean whereby it be wrought , namely , the preach , hear and read the word of God . they have not , neither will they have the scripture to be sound preach , read , and hear in their own tongue . that they can abide to have they in their own language , appear by this one example : one Pauier a town-clerk of London , ( in the time of king Henry the eightth ) hear that the scripture shall be put into english , he speak to this effect , and confirm it with a oath : viz. that if he know that the scripture shall be put into english , and that the king will have they to be read in the church , rather than he will live so long to see it , he will cut his own throat : but ( as hall say , who hear he speak it ) he be not so good as his word : for , in stead of cut his throat , he hang himself . C. what be the cause that they can abide to have the scripture in their own language ? m. S. John give the reason . for , every one that do evil , hate the light , neither come to the light , lest his deed shall be reprove and discover , John 3.20 . if the owl fly abroad by day , the bird by and by discern he , follow he and fall upon he ; and therefore he fly abroad usual in the night , and then he be quiet . if the scripture shall be suffer to be expound , and read of all nation in their own language , than that owl of roam ( the pope i mean ) with all his foolery and abomination , will be discern and discover ; and then the world will hate he ▪ follow after he , and persecute he , ( even as the small bird do the owl : ) and therefore they can abide the scripture in the vulgar tongue , but love darkness rather than light , because these deed be evil , Ioh. 3.19 . C. what say you then to their good work , as build of church , give of alm ? etc m. these and such like work , require in the law of God , in themselves be good , and to be practise of all : but to they they be ( as the father call the virtue of the heathen ) but splendidæ peccata , glitter dross , and beautiful deformity . C. do they teach any thing contrary to the moral law , and to the doctrine of good work ? m. yes , they do both teach and practise many thing direct contrary to God commandment . they teach for good work , such thing as be not command , but rather forbid in the law of God , as namely , to go on pilgrimage , to vow single life , to fast forty day and forty night , etc first , concern set pilgrimage unto certain image : there be none of the father that do so much as dream of they for 600. year after Christ at the least , Perkins 2. vol. page 541 , 542. second , the necessity of the vow of continency be establish first , and anner unto order , about 380. year after Christ , and that by pope Siricius . but it have no universal admission , until the time of pope Hildebrand , in the year 1070. Perkins 1. vol. 583. to 587. 2. volume 575 , 576. act and monument , 1151. third , their doctrine of single life be never command of God , nor know in the primitive church , but have spring up since , and be indeed the very doctrine of devil , 1. Tim. 4.13 . so be also their forbid of meat for religion sake . they teach also , that a man may fulfil the law ; yea , do work of supererogation , that be , more than the law do require : and that man of their abundance , may allot unto other such work of supererogation , Rhem. on 1. Cor. 9 sect . 6. 2. Cor. 8. sect . 3. this doctrine of they make the law of God to be unperfect , and be direct contrary to the word of Christ , luke 17.10 . and it be not know of the ancient father . they speak indeed sometime of supererogation , but in a far other sense then the papist doe . there be no such work to be find in the person of any mere man or angel , but only in the person of Christ , God and man , Perk . 1. vol. 598.599.2 . vol. 540.541 .

reason to show that the influence of the star be not evil . sure it can come from God , who be the sovereign good , and the first cause ordain all thing . for that be evil or sin , which be do against the will of the creator , or which decline from his justness : and in nature , evil can proceed from the spring of goodness . wherefore it shall be too foolish and absurd a thing to think , that evil shall take original from God allgood : and that from the sovereign governor shall proceed the violation of the law , see that his will be his own law it self . for the intelligence or angel , which some say continue in the motion of the sphere , and to who some assign the cause of evil influence : i will fain ask they , whether this intelligence do so work by the commandment of his prince , which be the eternal and sovereign governor of the world , or else against his will and ordinance ? if it accomplish any thing accord to his precept , certain that can be bad : for nothing be bad but that which be do contrary to the order establish by the prince of nature . but if it operate against his will , it may seem to turn to the scandal of the omnipotent creator , to suffer his servant so arrogant in rebellion unpunished . for at the beginning he spare not those among the angel , who exalt themselves in pride : and for the rest who persist in obedience , he in such sort confirm they , that they can sin any more . whereupon also ensue that the intelligence , be loyal servant of the eternal , do not cause evil influence , neither yet do dispose any thing to evil , since they always accomplish the ordinance of the soueraigne-good . against such as accuse the heaven to be evil . and for those , who not be able to blame God , nor the move intelligence , do accuse the heaven , as cause of evil influence : i desire they to tell i ; if the heaven be malicious , whence come their malice ? whether have they be create evil , or else be deprave by evil manner ? or else , their nature fail they , do they become evil ? certain they be not create evil by the most good worker . neither have they be deprave by manner or a fail nature , because they have no choice in their work , and for that their motion and influence be natural and invariable , not of freewill , as all philosopher affirm . again i ask they , whether the heaven give influence , as second cause , or as instrument ? if as instrument , then sith they neither move nor operate , but accord to the will of the prince , who create they ; he shall remain in fault : which be already confute . but if it be as second cause , see the work be perform by that which live , the error shall be refer to the intelligence , who move they , and be ordain for soul to the sphere : to which we have also satisfy . i will far add this point , that the heaven do work either by nature or by will . if by nature , sith the order thereof be establish by God , the sentence of Aristotle be worthy the remembrance , where say , [ what we possess by nature , we be therein neither praise nor blame : for the praise belong to the worker , and the blame can be assign to the order of the sovereign good . ] but if the heaven work free , be guide by a intelligence , it must needs follow , that be the cause of so many mischief , which abundant light on man , they be guide by a furious , or else by a covetous soul . if by a furious , see there be a certain planet , which ( as these astronomer say ) do always design violent influence , wherefore be it not consume with such a sempiternal fury ? moreover why say man , that a planet be inflame soon in one house , then in another ? what meet it in his own house or in another , that dispose it to signify evil ? again , how be that inflame with fury , which they affirm to be immutable ? but if the heaven be lead by covetousness , what profit or gain do they attract from thing here below ? for this covetous desire remain only in themselves . and whosoever covet evil , do it ( as the philosopher say ) because that he be as it be intoxicate and overcome by inebriate matter . but sith the matter of the heaven be far from all change and error , it be neither inebriate nor subvert . wherewith then will they blame the heaven ? if any say that by nature they be evil , reason and original of evil in nature . how be it , that this evil shall come into their nature ? for the sage collect these reason and original of evil in nature : either for that the efficient cause can govern the superfluity of the matter ; or else can supply where it fail ; or else can du dispose it ; and such other reason , as presuppose a feeble power of the cause . but for as much as the smith and carpenter of the heaven be of infinite power , how shall he fail in his workmanship ? for there be no other cause which may encounter he , since that without any aid , he frame the heaven ; neither can any thing resist the infinite worker , who reduce all thing into a due and proper estate , as we have heretofore ample declare . yet notwithstanding so many reason , how many be there which obstinate lean to the vain and curious observation of the star , as a infallible rule of the life and estate of man , do above all thing fear , lest in the ascendant of the nativity , any evil planet shall have domination ? and indeed do we not behold that astrologer , and above all magician , be so much seek after among we , that many do ( as one may say ) hold he to be miserable , who know not his horoscope ? to the end then that we may better expel out of the brain of every one such foolish opinion , let we ( i pray you ) stretch out this discourse in particular against such as accuse Saturn and mar for most evil planet : the charge whereof ( AMANA ) i commit to you .

much more i , his natural subject , link unto your highness , his natural son , with the same bond , rapt with the same admiration , feed with the same hope , must say , have finish these my labour , cui dicâre debeam ambigere nefas : for the same reason that move he , must likewise encourage i ; your greatness must encourage , because the same , be a princely branch spring from that royal stock ; your religion must encourage , because the same which your worthy father , and all the father of the primitive church , have make profession of ; your hopeful endeavour must encourage , because the same , for what he conceive of your princely father , the whole world expect shall be perform , if not by he , by your hand . his pen have make way for your sword , and his peace , if God give long life , may far your war : glorious be his peace and your war , and god glory the end of both . his highness can best judge what be fit ;
